title: The History of Radio
description: For questions 9 – 16, read the text below and think of the word which best fits each gap. Use only one word in each gap. There is an example at the beginning (0).

content.text: Radio revolutionised communication in the early 20th century, becoming (0) the first technology that could transmit voices and music wirelessly over long distances. Its impact on society was profound, transforming entertainment, news distribution, and emergency communications.||The invention of radio involved contributions (9) from several scientists and inventors. While Guglielmo Marconi is often credited with inventing radio, the technology built (10) on earlier work by scientists like Nikola Tesla and Heinrich Hertz. Marconi successfully demonstrated long-distance wireless communication in 1901, (11) when he transmitted a signal across the Atlantic Ocean.||In its early days, radio was primarily used (12) for ship-to-shore communication and military purposes. Commercial broadcasting began in the 1920s, and radio quickly became a mass medium. Families would gather around their radio sets to listen (13) to news, music, and drama programmes. Radio gave birth to new forms of entertainment and created the first media celebrities.||During World War II, radio played (14) a crucial role in propaganda and news dissemination. It remained the dominant form of home entertainment until television arrived in the 1950s. Many predicted radio would disappear, but (15) it adapted instead, focusing on music, news, and talk shows. Today, despite competition from streaming services and podcasts, traditional radio still reaches billions of listeners worldwide. Internet radio and digital broadcasting (16) have given the medium new life, ensuring its continued relevance in the digital age.

example.text: Radio revolutionised communication, becoming (0) first technology that could transmit voices wirelessly.

questions.explanation: Expresión 'contributions from scientists' indica el origen de las contribuciones.
Verbo 'built on' significa basarse en trabajo anterior.
Conjunción temporal que introduce el momento de la demostración.
Expresión 'used for purposes' indica el uso o propósito.
Expresión 'played a role' con artículo indefinido.
Pronombre sujeto que se refiere a la radio.
Presente perfecto: 'have given' indica resultado hasta el presente.
